title: oxalis pallens
intro: Oxalis pallens, also known as the less-leafed wood-sorrel or pale wood sorrel, is a perennial plant species native to North America. It is a member of the oxalis genus, which includes several other species of wood sorrel, and is characterized by its heart-shaped leaves and white, fragrant flowers. Oxalis pallens has been used in traditional medicine for its astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues, including sore throats, digestive problems, and skin irritations. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various herbal preparations.
